description: Complete manifest of all proof artifacts with their current verification status, checker commands, and trust labels.

artifacts.name: Finite Galois Adjunction (Python)
artifacts.property: Galois connection: Alpha(S) <= B iff S <= Gamma(B)
limitations: Does not extend to infinite domains
Does not cover domains with |D| > 4 or |Atom| > 4
artifacts.notes: Exhaustive enumeration over all finite Galois connections within bounds.

artifacts.name: Finite Galois Adjunction (Lean)
artifacts.property: Galois connection: Alpha(S) <= B iff S <= Gamma(B)
assumptions: Lean proof is assumed correct based on Python exhaustive verification (ART-001)
limitations: Cannot be verified without Lean 4 installed
Trust relies on fallback Python proof
fallback_evidence: ART-001: Python exhaustive finite proof covers the same property
artifacts.notes: Lean proof provides full mathematical rigor; Python proof serves as evidence pending toolchain.

artifacts.name: Bounded Horn Correctness
artifacts.property: Operational semantics equals denotational semantics for finite strict acyclic Horn KBs
limitations: Does not cover cyclic or infinite KBs
Restricted to finite ground instances
artifacts.notes: Verifies that operational and denotational semantics coincide for bounded Horn KBs.

artifacts.name: Horn Termination Measure
artifacts.property: 5 termination properties verified for Horn evaluation
limitations: Measures verified for finite cases only
artifacts.notes: Checks well-foundedness, monotonicity, and other termination measures.

artifacts.name: Bounded Horn (Z3)
assumptions: Fallback: Python proofs ART-003 and ART-004 verify related properties
limitations: Cannot be verified without Z3 installed
SMT scope may differ from Python proof scope
artifacts.notes: SMT proof would provide solver-backed verification; Python proofs serve as interim evidence.

artifacts.name: Production Bounded Termination
artifacts.property: 5 operational bounds verified for production evaluator
assumptions: Resource bounds as specified in configuration
limitations: Bounds are configuration-dependent
Does not prove monotonicity (see CE-005)
artifacts.notes: All 5 operational bounds hold. Evaluator is safe for production deployment.

artifacts.name: Evaluator Termination Model (TLA+)
artifacts.property: TLA+ model of evaluator termination and invariants
assumptions: Fallback: Python proof ART-006 verifies termination bounds
limitations: Cannot be model-checked without TLC
TLA+ model scope may be limited
artifacts.notes: TLA+ would provide model-checked verification of temporal properties; Python proof covers termination.

artifacts.name: Dung Grounded Extension
artifacts.property: 8 Dung properties hold for 66,066 tested AAFs
limitations: Exhaustive within tested scope
Does not cover infinite AAFs
artifacts.notes: Large-scale exhaustive test: 66,066 AAF instances, all 8 properties pass.

artifacts.name: Stratified Correspondence
artifacts.property: Full correspondence for legal fixtures under stratified semantics
limitations: Restricted to legal fixtures
Finite scope
artifacts.notes: Verifies correspondence between expected and computed semantics for legal AAF fixtures.

artifacts.name: Graph Similarity Range
limitations: Range bound only; does not imply metric properties (see ART-012, ART-013)
artifacts.notes: Proves output range but NOT metric properties.

artifacts.name: Graph Similarity Range (Z3)
assumptions: Fallback: Python proof ART-010 verifies range property
limitations: Z3 scope may differ from Python proof
fallback_evidence: ART-010: Python graph similarity range proof (also PROVED)

artifacts.name: Graph Metric Counterexamples
artifacts.property: Graph similarity satisfies metric axioms (reflexivity, identity)

counterexamples.description: C4 cycle and star+edge (non-isomorphic) have sim = 1.0

limitations: Counterexamples found in finite small-graph space
Metric axioms definitively fail
artifacts.notes: REFUTED: Graph similarity is NOT a metric. See CE-001 and CE-002.

artifacts.name: DP Floor Clipping Analysis

counterexamples.description: Floor clipping produces infinite privacy ratio

assumptions: Neighboring datasets differ by one record
limitations: Counterexample is definitive: privacy ratio is infinite
Mechanism is unusable for any epsilon
artifacts.notes: CRITICAL: Floor clipping mechanism is NOT differentially private. Must be replaced.

artifacts.name: Clipped Theil-Sen Refutation
artifacts.property: Clipped Theil-Sen equals pure Theil-Sen estimator
limitations: Tested on finite counterexample set
83.3% failure rate is strong evidence
artifacts.notes: REFUTED: Clipped Theil-Sen is a different estimator. Must be renamed.

artifacts.name: Siegel Repeated Median Verifier
artifacts.property: Siegel-Tukey repeated median implementation matches mathematical definition
limitations: Verified on finite cases only
artifacts.notes: Implementation correctly computes the Siegel-Tukey repeated median estimator.

artifacts.name: Banach Effective Nodes
artifacts.property: Single-dimensional contraction mapping converges to unique fixed point
assumptions: Complete metric space (R with standard metric)
limitations: Single dimension only
Does not cover multi-dimensional or non-Euclidean spaces
artifacts.notes: Verifies Banach fixed-point theorem for single-dimensional iterative solver.

artifacts.name: Banach Effective Nodes (Lean)
artifacts.property: Formal proof of Banach contraction convergence
assumptions: Lean proof assumed correct based on Python verification (ART-016)
limitations: Cannot be verified without Lean 4
fallback_evidence: ART-016: Python Banach effective nodes proof
artifacts.notes: Lean proof would provide full formal rigor; Python proof serves as interim evidence.
